title: Colliders, Rigidbodies, Layers, and Physics Queries
summary: Unity 3D physics fundamentals for code-generating tools: collider selection (Box/Sphere/Capsule/Mesh/Terrain/Wheel), Rigidbody dynamics vs kinematics, FixedUpdate semantics, collision vs trigger callbacks, layer-based collision filtering, raycast/overlap queries, physics materials, continuous collision detection, and joints. The two most common failure modes in generated physics code are applying forces in Update (framerate-coupled, non-deterministic) and moving kinematic Rigidbodies via transform.position (bypasses collision, causes tunneling). Read this before emitting any script that adds colliders, applies forces, or queries the physics scene.

llmGuidance.when_a_script_applies_forces_or_moves_a_: Put it in FixedUpdate, not Update. Use rb.AddForce / rb.MovePosition / rb.MoveRotation. Never assign transform.position on a Rigidbody — it teleports past colliders and desyncs the physics solver.
llmGuidance.when_generating_a_fast_moving_projectile: Set rb.collisionDetectionMode = CollisionDetectionMode.Continuous (for dynamic-vs-static) or ContinuousDynamic (for dynamic-vs-dynamic). Default Discrete tunnels through thin colliders at high velocity.
llmGuidance.when_adding_a_mesh_collider_to_a_moving_: Set convex = true. A non-convex MeshCollider combined with a non-kinematic Rigidbody throws at runtime. For concave static geometry, keep convex = false but do NOT attach a dynamic Rigidbody.
llmGuidance.when_making_two_object_groups_ignore_eac: Do NOT disable colliders. Assign each group to a Layer and uncheck their intersection in Project Settings > Physics > Layer Collision Matrix, or call Physics.IgnoreLayerCollision(a, b, true). Disabling colliders breaks raycasts too.
llmGuidance.when_detecting_overlap_every_frame: Use Physics.OverlapSphereNonAlloc (or OverlapBoxNonAlloc) with a pre-allocated Collider[] buffer. The allocating overloads create garbage every call and spike GC.
llmGuidance.when_a_kinematic_rigidbody_must_move_thr: Use rb.MovePosition(target) and rb.MoveRotation(q) inside FixedUpdate. Setting transform.position on a kinematic Rigidbody bypasses collision interpolation and causes sweep misses.
llmGuidance.when_raycasting_from_camera: Use Physics.Raycast(Camera.main.ScreenPointToRay(Input.mousePosition), out hit, maxDistance, layerMask). Don't build the ray from transform.forward.
llmGuidance.when_using_trigger_volumes: The GameObject with the script must have a Rigidbody (or the other side must). OnTriggerEnter fires only when at least one of the colliding pair has a Rigidbody. Static-vs-static triggers are silent.
llmGuidance.when_detecting_overlap_inside_update: Prefer FixedUpdate for physics queries or cache results from the last FixedUpdate. Calling OverlapSphere in Update before the first physics tick returns 0 because colliders have not yet registered with the broadphase.
llmGuidance.when_assigning_a_physics_material: Assign to .sharedMaterial, not .material. The .material getter CLONES the material per-collider on first access — this leaks if done in a hot path and breaks shared tuning.
llmGuidance.when_writing_2d_physics: Use Rigidbody2D + Collider2D (BoxCollider2D, CircleCollider2D, PolygonCollider2D). 3D Rigidbody and 2D Collider2D never interact. Do not mix.

colliderTypes.notes: Max 255 verts after convex reduction. Required for non-kinematic.
ILLEGAL with non-kinematic Rigidbody — throws on Awake.
Requires parent Rigidbody and Friction Curves.

fields.mass: kg. Only RELATIVE mass matters in collisions — 100:1 resolves like 1000:10.
fields.drag: Linear damping per second. 0 = vacuum; 0.05-0.2 typical.
fields.isKinematic: Not driven by forces. Moves only via script. Still collides with dynamic bodies and fires callbacks.
fields.interpolation: None | Interpolate | Extrapolate. Smooths physics-tick to render-frame gap. Enable for visible bodies.
fields.constraints: Freeze position/rotation per axis. Used for 2.5D or upright characters.

forceModes.effect: Continuous force (N). Uses mass and deltaTime. Default for AddForce.
Instant change in momentum. Uses mass, not deltaTime. Explosions, jumps.
Continuous, ignores mass. Good for constant gravity-like pushes.
Instant velocity delta, ignores mass and deltaTime.

addForceApis: rb.AddForceAtPosition(force, worldPos, ForceMode) — generates torque from offset
rb.AddExplosionForce(force, center, radius, upwardsModifier, ForceMode) — radial falloff

fixedUpdateSemantics.tickRate: Default 50 Hz (Time.fixedDeltaTime = 0.02s). Tune in Project Settings > Time.
fixedUpdateSemantics.catchUp: FixedUpdate fires 0..n times per rendered frame to catch up to wall time.
fixedUpdateSemantics.velocityWrites: Writing rb.velocity in Update is lossy. Always write velocity/forces in FixedUpdate.
fixedUpdateSemantics.interpolation: Interpolate smooths between previous and current physics state (one-tick visual lag). Extrapolate predicts ahead (no lag, overshoots at impact). None for static or invisible bodies.

collisionCallbacks: OnCollisionEnter(Collision c) — fires once when contact starts
OnCollisionStay(Collision c) — fires each FixedUpdate while in contact
OnCollisionExit(Collision c) — fires once when contact ends
rules: At least one side must have a Rigidbody for any callback to fire.
Callbacks fire on the GameObject whose MonoBehaviour defines them.
If either collider has isTrigger=true, OnTrigger* is used; otherwise OnCollision*.
Two static colliders never fire either set.
Two kinematic Rigidbodies fire only triggers by default, not collisions.

layersAndCollisionMatrix.layerModel: 32 layers (0-31). One layer per GameObject. Built-ins 0-5: Default, TransparentFX, IgnoreRaycast, Water, UI.
layersAndCollisionMatrix.matrix: Project Settings > Physics > Layer Collision Matrix. Unchecked pair skips broadphase.
runtimeApis: Physics.IgnoreCollision(a, b, true) — per-pair, forgotten on scene reload

raycastAndOverlap.raySingle: Physics.Raycast(origin, direction, out hit, maxDistance, layerMask, queryTriggerInteraction) — first hit.
raycastAndOverlap.rayNonAlloc: Physics.RaycastNonAlloc — fills caller buffer, returns count.
overlapQueries: NonAlloc variants fill a caller buffer

triggerInteraction.globalDefault: Physics.queriesHitTriggers defaults true. Pass the enum explicitly in generated code to avoid dependency on project settings.

raycastAndOverlap.performance: Alloc variants allocate per call; NonAlloc reuses caller buffer. Prefer NonAlloc in hot paths.

fields.staticFriction: Friction required to start sliding. Usually >= dynamicFriction.

sharedVsInstance.sharedMaterial: Direct reference; edits affect every collider using it.
sharedVsInstance.material: First read CLONES sharedMaterial; clones leak if not Destroy()d.
sharedVsInstance.rule: Use .sharedMaterial unless you explicitly need per-collider overrides.

physicsMaterial.className: Type is PhysicsMaterial (renamed from PhysicMaterial in Unity 6; 2021.3-2022.3 still use PhysicMaterial). Both names resolve the same asset.

modes.behavior: Default. Tunnels through thin colliders at high velocity.
Sweep vs static AND ContinuousDynamic bodies. Pair projectile + target for reliable catch.
Speculative contacts; cheaper, less accurate. Works vs all body types including kinematic. Good character default.

continuousCollisionDetection.whenToUse: Arrows, bullets, fast vehicles, falling characters. Cost: several-fold more than Discrete per Rigidbody.

types.useFor: Damped elastic tether between two bodies.
Every DOF Free/Limited/Locked with drives. Rebuilds any other joint.

commonFields: anchor / connectedAnchor — local pivots on each body
enableCollision — allow the two bodies to collide (default false)
joints.rule: Ragdolls → CharacterJoint. Vehicles → ConfigurableJoint with axes locked/sprung. Reach for ConfigurableJoint before stacking Hinge + Spring.

centerOfMassAndInertia.autoCompute: Auto-computed from attached colliders by volume.
centerOfMassAndInertia.override: Assign Rigidbody.centerOfMass (local) to shift balance — e.g., lower CoM for vehicle stability.
centerOfMassAndInertia.inertiaTensor: Vector3 of principal moments + inertiaTensorRotation. Override for unusual shapes.

sleepAndWake.purpose: Engine stops integrating bodies below the sleep threshold — saves CPU.
sleepAndWake.wakeOnStaticChange: Adding/moving/destroying static colliders at runtime rebuilds the broadphase and wakes nearby bodies. Batch static creation at load, not gameplay.
sleepAndWake.kinematicRule: Kinematic bodies always count as awake.

twoDVsThreeD.separateApis: Rigidbody2D + Collider2D (Box/Circle/Polygon/Edge/Capsule 2D) are a parallel API. 2D and 3D colliders never interact.
twoDVsThreeD.sceneSettings: Project Settings > Physics 2D has its own matrix and gravity (Vector2, default Y = -9.81).
twoDVsThreeD.commonMistake: Attaching BoxCollider2D in a 3D project — compiles but never collides.
twoDVsThreeD.rule: Pick one dimension per scene. 2.5D uses 3D Rigidbody with FreezePositionZ.

decisionRules.rule: In FixedUpdate with ForceMode.Force. Update causes framerate-dependent physics.

decisionRules.scenario: Move a kinematic Rigidbody without tunneling
decisionRules.rule: rb.MovePosition(target) in FixedUpdate. Never assign transform.position on a Rigidbody.
collisionDetectionMode = Continuous (or ContinuousDynamic if target also moves). Discrete tunnels when velocity*dt > collider thickness.
Pre-allocate Collider[] and use OverlapSphereNonAlloc. Allocating variants generate per-frame GC.

decisionRules.scenario: Groups must not physically interact but still receive raycasts
decisionRules.rule: Assign distinct Layers. Uncheck intersection in Layer Collision Matrix. Never disable colliders for this.
MeshCollider convex=true. Non-convex + non-kinematic Rigidbody throws on Awake.
Physics.Raycast(Camera.main.ScreenPointToRay(mouse), out hit, dist, mask). Never use transform.forward.

commonPitfalls.cause: Broadphase has not stepped; colliders not yet registered.
commonPitfalls.fix: Call Physics.SyncTransforms() first, or defer to the first FixedUpdate.

commonPitfalls.cause: LayerMask built wrong (layer index instead of 1 << index) or collision matrix unchecked.
commonPitfalls.fix: Use LayerMask.GetMask("A","B"). Check queriesHitTriggers if target is a trigger.

commonPitfalls.symptom: Rigidbody visibly jitters at steady framerate.
commonPitfalls.cause: Interpolation off, or forces applied in Update.
commonPitfalls.fix: Set interpolation = Interpolate; move all force writes to FixedUpdate.

commonPitfalls.symptom: Character with kinematic Rigidbody walks through walls.
commonPitfalls.cause: Moving via transform.position skips the physics sweep.

commonPitfalls.symptom: Runtime perf tanks after instantiating terrain chunks.
commonPitfalls.cause: Static colliders added at runtime rebuild the PhysX static BVH.
commonPitfalls.fix: Batch static creation at load, or use non-static meshes with kinematic Rigidbodies.

commonPitfalls.symptom: InvalidOperationException: Non-convex MeshCollider with non-kinematic Rigidbody.
commonPitfalls.fix: convex=true, OR isKinematic=true, OR swap to a primitive.

commonPitfalls.symptom: Physics material edits stop affecting other colliders.
commonPitfalls.cause: Touched Collider.material, which cloned on first access.
commonPitfalls.fix: Edit .sharedMaterial; Destroy() any clones on disable.

commonPitfalls.symptom: Triggers never fire between two static objects.
commonPitfalls.fix: Add a kinematic Rigidbody to one side.

commonPitfalls.symptom: 2D collider never collides in a 3D project.
commonPitfalls.fix: Pick one dimension; use 3D with FreezePositionZ for 2.5D.

commonPitfalls.symptom: Rigidbody sleeps mid-air and stops falling.
commonPitfalls.fix: Lower drag, call rb.WakeUp(), or raise Physics.sleepThreshold.

versionDeltas.change: ArticulationBody component added for chained rigid bodies (robotics, industrial). Solves joint chains with higher stability than Configurable Joint trains.
versionDeltas.migrationNote: Optional. Use when building articulated structures; keep Rigidbody for general gameplay.

versionDeltas.change: PhysX upgraded to 4.1 in the Unity 2021 cycle. Improved solver stability and CCD; some edge-case behavior differences vs 2020-era PhysX 4.0.
Unity.Physics (DOTS / Jobs-based deterministic physics, separate package) matures. Classic Rigidbody/PhysX remains the default; Unity.Physics is opt-in via ECS projects.
PhysicMaterial class renamed to PhysicsMaterial. Old name remains as a compatibility alias. Default queriesHitTriggers behavior unchanged (true).
versionDeltas.migrationNote: New code in Unity 6 should use PhysicsMaterial. Pre-6 code referring to PhysicMaterial continues to compile.

authorshipNotes: Content authored by Mosaic team from publicly-documented Unity APIs, Manual pages, and ScriptReference. Component names, enum values, API signatures, and numeric defaults (sleepThreshold, fixedDeltaTime) are factual. Explanatory prose, decision rules, llmGuidance, commonPitfalls, and version delta observations are original Mosaic editorial content. Unity Manual and ScriptReference pages are cited as externalReferences and NOT reproduced here. PhysX is NVIDIA's technology used under Unity's license; only behavioral facts are described, not internal algorithms.
